description: Self-leak firewall deny-list (F-198 / Proposal 205). Patterns matching Specrew-SELF facts that must never ship in consumer-deployed surfaces without an adjacent 'specrew-self-ok: <reason>' annotation. Single truth for the repo CI lane and the consumer-side checks. The lint excludes this file itself from scanning (it is the rule file).

annotation.semantics: A hit is annotated when the hit line, or the line immediately above it, contains the token followed by non-empty reason text. Comment form per file kind: <!-- specrew-self-ok: reason --> for .md; # specrew-self-ok: reason for .ps1/.psd1/.yml. A token with no reason text is treated as unannotated.

applicability_annotation.semantics: A stack-assumption or delivery-assumption match requires exactly one adjacent whole-comment marker: specrew-applicability: <kind>; <non-empty reason>. example-only also requires the statement itself to say example, illustrative, non-binding, or not a mandate. specrew-self-ok never suppresses an applicability finding.

entries.reason: Specrew's own publish registry. Consumer teaching must resolve the project's publish target from its governance, never name ours as the example-that-reads-as-mandate.
entries.source: proposal 205 W1 seed; 204-W7 field leak (2026-07-09 beta-1 E2E closeout taught PSGallery steps in a no-remote repo)

entries.reason: Specrew's own release SDLC. As deployed methodology it must be scoped to projects WITH a publish target, resolved at render time from the project's release model.
The self-repo forge slug; consumer surfaces must reference the consumer's own repository.
Specrew dev worktree naming. Reaching a consumer session it causes identity conflation (a downstream agent asserted 'This project is specrew-197-continuous-co-review' about a consumer project).
Maintainer dev-machine absolute paths; consumer surfaces must never carry the self-repo's location.
entries.source: proposal 205 W1 seed; 203 W2 sibling (origin-absolute paths are learnable from committed docs)

entries.reason: Specrew's own consumer test-project naming; a self-fact when it appears in deployed teaching.
entries.source: F-198 seed (field dogfood project names appear across self-host ledgers)

entries.reason: Specrew self feature identifiers read as consumer instruction; deployed teaching must stand on the abstract rule, not our feature history.
A Specrew self iteration identifier (the F-197 hardening iteration) named by the proposal as a leaked self-fact.
Self-repo proposal paths as consumer instruction; consumers cannot and should not read Specrew's proposal tree.
Specrew self decision-ledger identifiers; the generic .squad/decisions.md path is legitimate consumer layout, but OUR ledger entry ids are self-facts.
entries.source: F-198 seed (narrowed from the proposal's '.squad/decisions.md as consumer instruction' to avoid false positives on the consumer's own ledger)

entries.reason: Maintainer identifiers; consumer surfaces are maintainer-neutral.

A concrete runtime, platform, or test tool is presented as an instruction. Consumer-facing technology instructions require one adjacent applicability marker proving project detection, explicit profile selection, provider gating, or clearly non-binding example status.

entries.pattern: (?i)(?:\b(?:must|required)\b[^\r\n]{0,50}\b(?:(?-i:GitHub|GitLab|Bitbucket|Azure DevOps)|pull request|PR/MR|registry|package (?:registry|publication|release|install)|prerelease|stable promotion|release tag)\b|\b(?:use|run|install|push|publish|promote|open|create)\s+(?:(?:to|a|the)\s+)?(?:(?-i:GitHub|GitLab|Bitbucket|Azure DevOps)|pull request|PR/MR|registry|package (?:registry|publication|release|install)|prerelease|stable promotion|release tag)\b|\b(?:(?-i:GitHub|GitLab|Bitbucket|Azure DevOps)|pull request|PR/MR|registry|package (?:registry|publication|release|install)|prerelease|stable promotion|release tag)\b[^\r\n]{0,40}\b(?:must|required)\b)
entries.reason: A concrete forge, review, package, or release workflow is presented as an instruction. Consumer-facing delivery instructions require one adjacent applicability marker tied to project governance or explicit example-only wording.
